questions.question: Explain the difference between public, private, and protected access specifiers in C++ classes.
expectedPoints: Public members are accessible from anywhere
Private members are accessible only within the class
Protected members are accessible within the class and by derived classes
Default access is private for classes

questions.question: Discuss the importance of encapsulation in object-oriented programming and how access specifiers help achieve it.
expectedPoints: Encapsulation binds data and functions together
It hides implementation details from users
Protects data integrity through controlled access
Enables changes to implementation without affecting users
